title: Boat Rental in Broome: Costs, Routes and 2026 Tips
html:
Broome's turquoise waters and ancient coastlines make it a prime destination for boating. Setting off from Port of Broome, a half-day charter of the Azimut 58 costs from €750, and you can compare options on BoatHire24.

Where to set off in Broome

Boating in Broome starts from several key spots. Port of Broome is the main harbour, with pontoons and fuelling facilities. Roebuck Bay offers calm waters for sheltered cruising, while Cable Beach is a famous stretch with turquoise shallows. Gantheaume Point provides a dramatic red-rock backdrop. For island trips, head to the Lacepede Islands, a 30-nautical-mile voyage north. The Dampier Peninsula, with its remote beaches and Aboriginal communities, is a popular longer excursion, accessible from the port.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht (e.g. Azimut 58), Ideal for luxury day trips; sleeps up to 12 guests; from €1,500 per half-day.
  2. Catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 42), Spacious for family or group cruising; up to 10 guests; from €1,800 per full day.
  3. Runabout (e.g. Axopar 28), Perfect for exploring the bay and getting to beaches; up to 8 guests; from €500 for a half-day.
  4. Skippered day charter, All-inclusive with a local skipper; up to 6 guests; from €750 for 4 hours.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Broome?

Rental prices in Broome range from €80 for a shared tour to €2,500 for a fully crewed yacht for the day. Here is a price-band breakdown:

  • Shared sunset tour: €80, €120 per person, typically 2 hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day: €250, €500, for up to 6 guests, no skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day: €1,200, €2,000, for up to 12 guests, skipper included.
  • Crewed yacht (Azimut 58 or similar): €1,500, €2,500 per day, all-inclusive.

You can find a skippered charter to suit any budget and compare verified boat listings.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Broome?

In Western Australia, a boat licence is required to operate a vessel over 4.5 kW on your own; skippered charters include a licensed skipper. Renting a bareboat without a licence is not possible for most craft, but many boats on BoatHire24 come with a skipper. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking. For licence holders, self-drive options like the Axopar 28 are available.

Visit the Horizontal Falls in a high-speed tour for an unforgettable experience; the tidal phenomenon creates a unique waterfall effect between two gorges, best seen from Broome.

At a glance

  • Price range: €80, €2,500 per day
  • Peak months: May to October (dry season)
  • Typical trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 12
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Broome

Can I rent a boat in Broome without a licence?

Yes, you can book a skippered charter where the operator provides a licensed skipper, so no licence is needed from you.

What is the best month to rent a boat in Broome?

The dry season from May to October offers the best weather, with calm seas and clear skies, making it ideal for boating.

How far is the Lacepede Islands by boat from Broome?

The Lacepede Islands are approximately 30 nautical miles north of Broome, a trip of about 1.5 hours by powerboat.

What is included in a crewed yacht charter?

A crewed yacht charter typically includes a skipper, crew, fuel, food, and drinks, and often snorkelling equipment and towels.

To understand the booking process, read how boat rental works on BoatHire24, including payment security and cancellation policies.

Consider renting the Azimut 58 for hire for a luxurious day exploring Broome's coastline. To get started, check availability on BoatHire24. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Broome and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
